verses.1: After this I saw another angel descending from heaven with great authority, and the earth was illuminated by his glory.
verses.2: And he cried out in a mighty voice:
verses.4: Then I heard another voice from heaven say:
verses.9: Then the kings of the earth who committed sexual immorality and lived in luxury with her will weep and wail at the sight of the smoke rising from the fire that consumes her.
verses.10: In fear of her torment, they will stand at a distance and cry out:
verses.11: And the merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, because there is no one left to buy their cargo—
verses.12: cargo of gold, silver, precious stones, and pearls; of fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; of all kinds of citron wood and every article of ivory, precious wood, bronze, iron, and marble;
verses.13: of cinnamon, spice, incense, myrrh, and frankincense; of wine, olive oil, fine flour, and wheat; of cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; of bodies and souls of slaves.
verses.15: The merchants who sold these things and gained their wealth from her will stand at a distance, in fear of her torment. They will weep and mourn,
verses.17: Every shipmaster, passenger, and sailor, and all who make their living from the sea, will stand at a distance
verses.18: and cry out at the sight of the smoke rising from the fire that consumes her. “What city was ever like this great city?” they will exclaim.
verses.19: Then they will throw dust on their heads as they weep and mourn and cry out:
verses.21: Then a mighty angel picked up a stone the size of a great millstone and cast it into the sea, saying:
verses.24: And there was found in her the blood of prophets and saints, and of all who had been slain on the earth.
